questions.question: What is the difference between acute inflammation and chronic inflammation?

options.text: There is no difference; all inflammation is harmful
Acute inflammation is a healthy healing response, while chronic inflammation is a persistent low-level fire that disrupts body systems
Chronic inflammation only affects joints, while acute inflammation affects the brain
Acute inflammation lasts for years, while chronic inflammation resolves quickly

questions.explanation: Short-term (acute) inflammation is a healthy healing response, like when you cut your finger. Chronic inflammation is like a fire alarm stuck on -- a persistent, body-wide condition that sends constant stress signals and disrupts every system, especially the brain.

questions.question: Which of the following dietary factors is a major contributor to chronic inflammation?

options.text: Eating whole fruits and vegetables
Consuming fermented foods like yogurt
Eating ultra-processed foods with long ingredient lists of artificial additives
Drinking adequate water throughout the day

questions.explanation: Ultra-processed foods with long ingredient lists of artificial additives can be seen as foreign invaders by your gut, triggering your immune system to stay on high alert and promoting chronic inflammation.

questions.question: What does recent research suggest about the relationship between inflammation and depression?

options.text: Inflammation and depression are completely unrelated
Depression causes inflammation, but inflammation does not cause depression
People experiencing depression often have higher levels of inflammatory markers, suggesting depression may be linked to body-wide inflammation
Only severe inflammation affects mood; mild inflammation has no impact

questions.explanation: Groundbreaking research shows that people experiencing depression often have higher levels of inflammatory markers in their blood. This suggests that for many, depression may not just be a chemical imbalance but a symptom of body-wide inflammation affecting the brain.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach to an anti-inflammatory diet according to the lesson?

options.text: Strictly eliminate all inflammatory foods immediately
Only eat raw vegetables and fruits
Crowd out inflammatory foods with delicious, whole, nutrient-dense alternatives
Follow a very low-calorie diet to reduce inflammation

questions.explanation: The lesson emphasizes that an anti-inflammatory diet is not about perfection or strict elimination, but about crowding out inflammatory foods with delicious, whole, nutrient-dense ones.

questions.question: Which of the following would be part of a brain-calming, anti-inflammatory plate?

options.text: Fried chicken, white bread, and soda
Salmon, spinach, avocado, and broccoli
A candy bar, chips, and a milkshake
Instant noodles with hot sauce

questions.explanation: A brain-calming, anti-inflammatory plate includes lean protein (salmon), leafy greens (spinach), healthy fats (avocado), and colorful vegetables (broccoli).
